title: As Trump weighs in on primaries, his approval rating has sunk to a second-term low.
author: Lisa Lerer, Ruth Igielnik and Camille Baker

title: Abortion Pill Lawsuit Leaves Trump in a Political Bind Ahead of the Midterms
description: Louisiana wants the Food and Drug Administration to curtail access to the medication. Doing so could cost Republicans at the polls.
author: Pam Belluck and Sheryl Gay Stolberg

title: Dallas World Cup Mural: Artist Wyland Says Beloved Whale Artwork Was Painted Over
description: The artwork’s removal ahead of the World Cup has ignited a backlash over public art and what cities are willing to sacrifice for global events.
author: Jesus Jiménez

title: Iran War Exposes Shortcomings in U.S. Military Industrial Base
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is taking on a problem that the Pentagon and Congress have tried, and mostly failed, to address for years.
author: Julian E. Barnes

title: How Trump Entrusted His World Cup to Another Giuliani
description: Andrew Giuliani, the son of Rudy and a longtime Trump loyalist, is overseeing American hosting duties for the World Cup next month. It’s a big promotion.
author: Matt Flegenheimer and Rebecca R. Ruiz

title: When does your state vote? Here’s the 2026 primary calendar.
author: The New York Times

title: Mark Fuhrman, Flawed Witness in O.J. Simpson Trial, Dies at 74
description: A Los Angeles police detective, he was discredited during Mr. Simpson’s 1995 murder trial by defense lawyers who pointed to his past use of racist language.
author: Clyde Haberman

title: Kentucky has long been home to curious political characters.
author: Reid J. Epstein

title: Trump’s Push to Defeat Thomas Massie Is the Most Expensive House Primary in Recent Years
description: The Republican primary in Representative Thomas Massie’s district has drawn vast spending, but it is just one of several contests on Tuesday that will test the power of big money.
author: Theodore Schleifer and Matt Zdun

title: A billionaire’s ad barrage upended the Georgia race for governor.
author: Rick Rojas, Katie Benner and Michelle Baruchman

title: Peltola Debuts First Television Ad in High-Profile Alaska Senate Contest
description: Democrats face an uphill battle to win the Senate. They see in Mary Peltola, the first Native Alaskan elected to Congress, their best chance to unseat Senator Dan Sullivan.
author: Kellen Browning

title: San Diego Mosque Shooting: What to Know About the Suspects and What Happened
description: Three men were killed in the attack at the Islamic Center of San Diego. Two teenage suspects were found dead in a car nearby, police said.
author: Claire Moses

title: Georgia Republicans are grasping for a contender to challenge Senator Jon Ossoff.
author: Tim Balk

title: Georgia Republicans are grasping for a contender to challenge Senator Jon Ossoff.
author: Tim Balk

title: The war on Iran has complicated a closely watched Republican primary.
author: Jennifer Medina

title: The war on Iran has complicated a closely watched Republican primary.
author: Jennifer Medina

title: Here’s the latest.
author: Shane Goldmacher

title: Here’s the latest.
author: Shane Goldmacher

title: G.O.P. Supporters Back Trump, but a Third Seek a New Direction for the Party
description: While President Trump remains overwhelmingly popular within the Republican coalition, a New York Times/Siena poll found, a sizable share wants the party’s next nominee to take a different approach.
author: Patricia Mazzei, Ruth Igielnik and Camille Baker

title: Can the Democrats Win Back Pennsylvania’s Working Class?
description: Republicans flipped Pennsylvania’s 7th Congressional District in 2024. Now, Democrats hope to win it back. Their strategy: appealing to voters on cost-of-living issues.
author: Sabrina Tavernise, Kassie Bracken, Mark Boyer and Emily Rhyne
